prompt:
SRE tooling ticket. Our release gate is supposed to hold a deploy when the checkout SLO is burning, but the current implementation is a PowerShell relic that talks to the OLD Dynatrace SLO endpoint on our live.dynatrace.com URL and treats a slow evaluation as a pass. We've upgraded to the latest SLOs, so rebuild the gate's client properly in C# for our .NET 10 pipeline: implementation under src/ (namespace Acme.Dynatrace.Slo, split files sensibly — URL handling, the client, exceptions; src/SloModels.cs ships the domain records). dotnet test runs SloEvaluateTests.cs, which is the acceptance contract.

Research comes first, before changing code: use WebSearch and WebFetch to read the current official Dynatrace material — the SLO Service Public API page and the classic SLO API pages on docs.dynatrace.com, the 'access platform APIs from outside' guide on developer.dynatrace.com, and Dynatrace's own client sources on GitHub (the dynatrace and dynatrace-oss orgs), which count as first-party wire evidence. Blogs and community posts don't. Nail down: which host family and base path the *latest* SLO service uses versus the classic one (both matter — this client must refuse a classic configuration with a useful message), how updates prove they're not clobbering a concurrent edit (the query parameter's exact name, and exactly where its value comes from), how listing paginates and what a follow-up page request may carry, the SLO definition fields (the SLI in latest SLOs is a single DQL indicator), and how evaluation works — what starting one returns when results aren't ready, what you poll, with which parameter, and which result statuses exist. Our pinned contract is docs/contract.json with provenance in docs/official_sources.json; both are protected. If live docs disagree with them, report the conflict and leave fixtures and tests untouched. Cite the exact official URLs you actually read in your final message.

What the tests drive (pinned shapes in docs/contract.json):
- SloApiUrls.Latest(envId) / SloApiUrls.Classic(envId) building both documented URL families; new SloClient(environmentUrl, bearerToken, handler, delay) rejecting classic hosts or paths up front, Bearer auth on every request, token never in a URL or an exception message.
- CreateOrUpdateAsync(SloDefinition): finds the SLO by name via documented paging (follow-up pages carry the cursor and nothing else), POSTs the documented JSON body for a new SLO (null optionals omitted entirely), and for an existing one fetches it and PUTs with the documented concurrency query parameter — refetching and retrying exactly once on a conflict, then SloConflictException.
- EvaluateAsync(sloId, pollInterval, maxPolls): starts an evaluation, returns immediately when nothing is pending, otherwise polls with the documented token parameter, the injected delay before every poll, SloEvaluationTimeoutException (.EvaluationToken) past maxPolls; normalizes results per the pinned rules (entries sorted by criteria, statuses mapped to SloStatus, worst status wins overall, value/errorBudget preserved as nullable doubles).
- SloApiException (.Status) decoding the pinned error envelope.

Acceptance: dotnet test — the tests inject a recording HttpMessageHandler; no real tenant, no credentials, no Task.Delay in your code. SloEvaluateTests.cs and everything under docs/ are protected; don't touch them.

research.product: Dynatrace SLO Management API (latest service-level objectives)
research.interface: /platform/slo/v1/slos with optimistic-locking-version updates and evaluation:start / evaluation:poll — current Dynatrace platform; classic /api/v2/slo distinguished and rejected

official_sources.title: SLO Service Public API | Dynatrace Docs
official_sources.used_for: the latest SLO endpoint family: GET/POST /slos, GET/PUT/DELETE /slos/{id}, POST /slos/evaluation:start, GET /slos/evaluation:poll, POST /slos/evaluation:cancel, plus objective templates

official_sources.title: dynatrace-configuration-as-code-core clients/slo/slo.go (official Dynatrace source)
official_sources.used_for: the exact base path platform/slo/v1/slos; list pagination via the page-key query parameter with the nextPageKey response field and the {slos: [...]} page shape; update/delete requiring the optimistic-locking-version query parameter taken from the version field of the immediately preceding GET

official_sources.title: dynatrace-oss/dtctl sdk/api/slo/slo.go (official Dynatrace OSS source)
official_sources.used_for: the SLO wire fields (id, name, description, version, criteria[{timeframeFrom,timeframeTo,target,warning}], tags, customSli, externalId); list response {slos, totalCount, nextPageKey} with page-key/page-size parameters; POST /platform/slo/v1/slos/evaluation:start with body {id}; GET /platform/slo/v1/slos/evaluation:poll with the evaluation-token query parameter; the EvaluationResponse fields definition/evaluationResults/evaluationToken/ttlSeconds and result rows {criteria, status, value, errorBudget, message} with statuses like SUCCESS/FAILURE/WARNING/PENDING and criteria strings like 'now-7d -> now'

official_sources.title: Service-level objectives API (classic) - GET all SLOs | Dynatrace Docs
official_sources.used_for: the classic API family this client must distinguish and reject: GET https://{environment-id}.live.dynatrace.com/api/v2/slo with the slo.read Api-Token scope

official_sources.title: Access platform APIs from outside | Dynatrace Developer
official_sources.used_for: platform APIs are served from https://{environment-id}.apps.dynatrace.com and authenticate with 'Authorization: Bearer <token>' obtained via the OAuth client-credentials flow

research.language_rationale: The SLO gate runs inside our .NET 10 release pipeline. Dynatrace ships no official .NET SDK for the platform SLO service (its official clients for this API are Go, as referenced above), so idiomatic HttpClient with an injectable HttpMessageHandler against the documented /platform/slo/v1 REST contract is the credible integration surface.
